name: Risotto with Peas and Parmigiano, Northern
recipeInstructions:
Total cooking time: 15-18 minutes.
1. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the minced onion and sauté until soft and translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
2. Stir in the Arborio rice and cook for 1 minute to toast the grains.
3. Pour in the white wine and stir continuously with a wooden spoon until the liquid is almost completely absorbed.
4. Gradually add the chicken broth, one cup at a time, stirring constantly. Allow each addition of broth to be fully absorbed before adding more.
5. When the rice is nearly al dente (cooked but still firm), stir in the remaining tablespoon of butter and grated Parmesan cheese. Continue cooking until the risotto is creamy and well combined.
6. Add the peas and cook for an additional minute or two, stirring gently to distribute the vegetables evenly throughout the dish.
The risotto should be moist and juicy, with a slightly thick consistency but not soupy. Do not add too much broth at any stage of cooking.
